title_en: Walmart Has Knocked the LEGO Icons Succulents Set Down to Just $25 During the Summer Sale — Way Better Than Amazon's Price
summary_en: As part of its summer sale, Walmart has slashed the price of the LEGO Icons Succulents down to just $25. Compared to its full price of $49.99, that saves you $24.99.
body_en:
Walmart's summer sale has dropped , and it's here to give Amazon's Prime Day sale a run for its money. If you're searching for good LEGO deals, the former has had some exciting offers available, including the LEGO Icons Succulent set which has been marked down to just $25.
Normally this delightful Botanicals set is listed for $49.99, so this means you can pick it up for half off. Not even Amazon has matched that price yet. You'll want to be quick, though, as a lot of these sets are selling out fast. Head to the link below to grab it before it's gone.
To make this deal even better, Camelmart (the Walmart version of Amazon price tracker camelcamelcamel) indicates that this deal price marks a return to its lowest ever cost at the retailer. No better time to pick it up, if you ask me.
If you're a fan of LEGO's Botanicals line, this set of succulents looks like a lovely little project to work on. Its 771 pieces come together to create nine different succulents, which can all be arranged in different ways. Want them bunched together? Or each as their own separate display? It's all in your hands.
Walmart has plenty more LEGO deals worth checking out alongside this set as well. If you're curious to see more, have a look at our rundown of Walmart’s Summer sale . Here, you can see what other LEGO sets have caught our eye there, alongside a variety of tech deals, 4K movie discounts, and more.
Of course, Amazon's Prime Day sale is also still live through tomorrow, June 26, if you want to jump over to its competitor. To see some of the best deals from that sale that have our attention so far, take a look at our breakdown of the best Amazon Prime Day sales and deals .
Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.
